query: microtasks vs macrotasks javascript interview question

competitors.label: Using microtasks in JavaScript with queueMicrotask()
competitors.selectionReason: This MDN guide is the clearest canonical explanation of how the event loop drains microtasks, how tasks differ, and why recursive microtasks can starve progress.
competitors.winReason: MDN is the stronger API guide, but our page is now better on the interview-sized production story: why a spinner may never paint, when to yield with a macrotask, and how browser rendering fits into the queue priority rule.
theirStrengths: It clearly documents the timing rules for tasks versus microtasks and the starvation risk from recursive microtasks.
theirGaps: It is more API-guide shaped than interview-compressed.

realWorldUseCases: That is why a loading spinner can stay invisible if a recursive Promise chain keeps the current turn alive.
followUpConfusion: Browsers may render between macrotasks, but not while the microtask queue is still draining.

realWorldUseCases: MDN connects microtask draining to rendering, but our page is now more concrete about the visible UI failure where a spinner never paints after user input.
